ksa_text: Knowledge of data classification tools and techniques, including sensitivity classification (public, internal, confidential), risk-based data categorization, classification policy implementation, and use in access control and protection strategies.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of functionality, quality, and security requirements and how these will apply to specific items of supply (i.e., elements and processes).

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of performance tuning tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of performance tuning tools and techniques
Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Data Quality falls outside the primary competency scope of the VP Analytics. This knowledge KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Data Quality truly out-of-scope for the VP Analytics? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Data Quality as a core or recommended competency for the VP Analytics. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Data Quality falls outside the established competency profile for the VP Analytics. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Data Quality is outside the competency scope of the VP Analytics. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
